steps.title: Spot the pattern in both examples
steps.content: Both examples follow an identical trajectory: an Indigenous language word → Spanish → English. This repeating structure reveals Spanish's role as a consistent intermediary.

steps.title: Apply the pattern to the choices
steps.content:
B identifies Spanish as a “medium”—a conduit or intermediary—through which Indigenous languages have influenced English.

This matches the examples, which show Indigenous words flowing through Spanish into English.

The other three claim quantitative parity or reciprocal borrowing absent from the passage.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
